title: cerastium alpinum subsp. pyrenaicum
intro: Cerastium alpinum subsp. pyrenaicum, also known as the Pyrenean mouse-ear or Pyrenean chickweed, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cerastium alpinum subsp. pyrenaicum include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and acting as a natural antiseptic for wound care, as well as helping to alleviate respiratory issues and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs, and can be consumed as a tea or used topically as a poultice or salve.
